evals.prompt: Use $knowledge to create a repo-local .knowledge folder for a new project so I have sources, knowledge, and rules layers with starter note templates.
evals.expected_output: A new .knowledge tree exists with the managed starter files and templates, and the validator accepts it.
assertions: The .knowledge root is created under the target repo.
The sources, knowledge, and rules layers exist.
The structure home note and all five note templates exist.
The scaffold summary reports apply mode with non-zero created directories.
The validator accepts the generated workspace.

evals.prompt: Use $knowledge to refresh the .knowledge scaffold in a repo that already has user notes and a customized managed file, but do not overwrite existing work.
evals.expected_output: Existing user content and customized starter files are preserved when the scaffold is re-run without force.
assertions: A pre-existing user-authored permanent note remains unchanged.
A customized managed file remains unchanged without --force-managed.
The script summary reports apply mode with skipped files.

evals.prompt: Use $knowledge to repair the managed starter files in an existing .knowledge folder while leaving user-authored notes intact.
evals.expected_output: Managed files are restored with --force-managed and user-authored files are preserved.
assertions: A customized managed file is rewritten to the default content when --force-managed is used.
A user-authored permanent note remains unchanged after the forced refresh.
The script summary reports apply mode with written files.

evals.prompt: Use $knowledge to verify that a damaged .knowledge workspace is invalid and identify the missing managed file.
evals.expected_output: The validator fails when a required managed file is missing and reports the missing file path.
assertions: The validator exits non-zero for a damaged workspace.
The validator reports the missing managed file.
The validator summary marks the workspace invalid.

evals.prompt: Use $knowledge to evaluate whether a set of example notes follows atomicity, provenance, and link-context expectations.
evals.expected_output: The note-quality checker accepts the example notes and reports a strong mean score.
assertions: The note-quality checker exits successfully for the good examples.
The note-quality summary marks the note set valid.
The note-quality summary reports five checked notes.
The mean quality score is at least 90.

evals.prompt: Use $knowledge to detect a bad permanent note that violates atomicity, provenance, and link-context expectations.
evals.expected_output: The note-quality checker fails and reports the quality problems in the damaged note.
assertions: The note-quality checker exits non-zero for the bad note.
The note-quality summary marks the note set invalid.
The bad note reports an atomicity error.
The bad note reports a link-context or provenance error.

evals.prompt: Use $knowledge to find what the repo already knows about atomic notes.
evals.expected_output: The search helper returns relevant knowledge notes about atomic notes with ranked results and snippets.
assertions: The search helper exits successfully for a relevant query.
The search helper returns at least two matches.
The top result path contains the atomic-notes topic.
The top result includes at least one snippet.

evals.prompt: Use $knowledge to find the rule that explains provenance behavior in the .knowledge system.
evals.expected_output: The search helper can search rules when requested and returns the provenance rule.
assertions: The search helper exits successfully when include-rules is enabled.
The results contain the provenance rule file.

evals.prompt: Use $knowledge to search for a topic that the repo does not know yet.
evals.expected_output: The search helper returns zero matches cleanly for an unknown topic.
assertions: The search helper exits successfully for an unknown query.
The search helper returns zero matches.

evals.prompt: Use $knowledge to record a knowledge update after a repo change and create a log entry.
evals.expected_output: The log writer creates a timestamped entry under logs/ and records the changed notes and repo sources.
assertions: The log writer exits successfully.
The log writer returns a logs/ relative path.
The log file exists.
The log file records the changed note path.
The log file records the repo source.

evals.prompt: Use $knowledge to find a previous knowledge update in the logs.
evals.expected_output: The search helper can include log entries for history questions and returns the matching log file.
assertions: The search helper exits successfully when include-logs is enabled.
The results contain the new log entry.

evals.prompt: Use $knowledge to answer from stored knowledge in natural prose and cite note files relative to the .knowledge root.
evals.expected_output: The skill instructions and retrieval example both enforce natural prose, avoid stiff note-system phrasing, and use .knowledge-root-relative source paths.
assertions: The skill instructions require direct natural prose for retrieval answers.
The skill instructions explicitly avoid meta phrases about the notes or knowledge base.
The retrieval example uses a Sources used section.
The retrieval example cites knowledge/ or rules/ paths instead of .knowledge/ paths.

evals.prompt: Use $knowledge to let an OpenAI judge review example notes for semantic note quality.
evals.expected_output: When an API key is available, the OpenAI judge accepts the good example set with a strong mean score.
assertions: The OpenAI judge exits successfully for the good examples.
The OpenAI judge marks the note set valid.
The OpenAI judge reports five checked notes.
The OpenAI judge mean score is at least 4.0.

evals.prompt: Use $knowledge to let an OpenAI judge detect a semantically weak note.
evals.expected_output: When an API key is available, the OpenAI judge rejects the bad note and reports concrete issues.
assertions: The OpenAI judge exits non-zero for the bad note.
The OpenAI judge marks the note set invalid.
The OpenAI judge reports at least one issue for the bad note.
